belief: Strengthening the Dutch AI ecosystem is an important strategic objective for KickstartAI.
belief_statement: Strengthening the Dutch AI ecosystem is an important strategic objective for KickstartAI.
source_quote: strengthening the Dutch AI ecosystem
source_excerpt: strengthening the Dutch AI ecosystem
source_text:
We’re excited to share that Erick Webbe will be joining KickstartAI as our new CEO starting January 1 🚀 

Erick is a familiar and trusted force within KickstartAI. As a long-time board member representing Ahold Delhaize, he has actively supported our mission to accelerate the adoption of AI in the Netherlands. He brings a deep understanding of how large organizations work, how AI moves from idea to implementation, and what it takes to connect different stakeholders around one shared goal.

In his new role as CEO, Erick will focus on growing KickstartAI’s impact: helping more organisations tackle concrete AI challenges, sharing what works openly with the community, and strengthening the Dutch AI ecosystem. 🇳🇱 

As Erick puts it, “the Netherlands must move from talking about AI to doing AI.”
“We want to show that progress doesn’t start with big words, but with tangible actions. And that a small country, if it wants to, can set a big example. I’m incredibly excited to turn our mission into reality.”

🌟 We’re looking forward to this next chapter with Erick guiding the way!

If you’d like to dive deeper into Erick’s background and vision, you can read his full interview below (link in comments) 👇 

#KickstartAI #AIAdoption #AINetherlands

belief: Helping customers improve their financial wellbeing and resilience is an important goal for ING.
belief_statement: Helping customers improve their financial wellbeing and resilience is an important goal for ING.
source_quote: an important step for ING in helping customers improve their financial wellbeing... and strengthen financial knowledge and resilience.
source_excerpt: an important step for ING in helping customers improve their financial wellbeing... and strengthen financial knowledge and resilience.
source_text:
🚀 The KickstartAI x ING Challenge is a wrap! 

Over a two-week sprint, 14 ML engineers and data scientists from KickstartAI, ING, and our partners KLM Royal Dutch Airlines, Nederlandse Spoorwegen, Ahold Delhaize, and bol joined forces to take on a bold mission: create a prototype for a financial health chatbot. 

🌟 And the outcome?

The team delivered a working prototype that operates anonymously and offers personalized educational support. It marks an important step for ING in helping customers improve their financial wellbeing, support those facing financial stress, and strengthen financial knowledge and resilience.

🇳🇱 This challenge proved once again the importance of sharing knowledge across industries and building collectively, ultimately accelerating AI adoption in the Netherlands. 

#AI #FinancialHealth #KickstartAI #ING

While the details stay under wraps, one thing is clear: when teams come together to openly exchange knowledge, the entire AI ecosystem grows stronger.

👏 A big thank you to everyone who contributed, shared their insights, and helped make the summit such a success.

source_excerpt: 🌎 How can multimodal AI advance biodiversity research?
source_text:
🌎 How can multimodal AI advance biodiversity research? 

In our latest article, we dive into two insightful projects: BioAnalyst, a large-scale foundation model for biodiversity forecasting, and bio-inspired computer vision to identify individual animals by the patterns on their skin.

We speak with Thanasis Trantas, AI Research Scientist at TNO, and Rita Pucci, Assistant Professor at LIACS about how these models are built, the challenges behind them, and what they could mean for the future of biodiversity research.

belief: Benchmark saturation changes the meaning and interpretability of accuracy scores in AI evaluation.
belief_statement: Benchmark saturation changes the meaning and interpretability of accuracy scores in AI evaluation.
source_quote: How benchmark saturation changes what accuracy scores actually represent
source_excerpt: How benchmark saturation changes what accuracy scores actually represent
source_text:
📖 In our latest article, we take a closer look at the limits of benchmark-driven AI research – and why today’s scores often tell us less than we think.

We spoke with Maarten C. Stol, Chief Scientific Officer at Braincreators, about how benchmarks like ImageNet shaped modern AI, but also narrowed what we consider progress.

We dive into: 
🔸 How benchmark saturation changes what accuracy scores actually represent
🔸 Why what models get wrong is more revealing than what they get right
🔸 Shifting evaluation from performance numbers to the quality and nature of error cases

👇 Read the full article below and share your thoughts in the comments!
https://lnkd.in/euQybT5N

belief: Accelerating the Dutch economy through AI requires shared learning and collective action among organisations.
belief_statement: Accelerating the Dutch economy through AI requires shared learning and collective action among organisations.
source_quote: KickstartAI is built on the belief that accelerating the Dutch economy through AI requires shared learning and action.
source_excerpt: KickstartAI is built on the belief that accelerating the Dutch economy through AI requires shared learning and action.
source_text:
Yesterday, ING and KickstartAI - together with our partners Ahold Delhaize, KLM Royal Dutch Airlines and Nederlandse Spoorwegen - hosted the AI team of the Ministry of Economic Affairs for a working visit focused on practical AI application and adoption.

🇳🇱 KickstartAI is built on the belief that accelerating the Dutch economy through AI requires shared learning and action. During the session, our founding partners shared practical insights from the AI projects we’re carrying out together: 
🔸 What it really takes to move from idea to implementation and adoption
🔸  How to set clear goals, and how to embed AI into the core of their organisations - not as side projects, but as drivers of impact.

We discussed how organisations can strengthen their AI adoption by learning through doing, by looking at AI not only as a technology but also in terms of organisational change and broader impact, and by integrating it deeply into customer-facing and operational processes. 

We also explored how the government can help lower barriers and accelerate this transition.

⚡ Our priority at KickstartAI is clear: the urgency of AI adoption is real, and action is needed now for the Netherlands to stay competitive, economically resilient, and technologically independent.

Thanks to the Ministry for the open and constructive exchange, and to our partners for sharing their experiences so openly. The work continues!

#AI #AINetherlands #AIAdoption

belief: The National AI Delta Plan is essential for making the Netherlands a European leader in AI.
belief_statement: The National AI Delta Plan is essential for making the Netherlands a European leader in AI.
source_quote: a concrete roadmap to make the Netherlands a European leader in AI… We believe the AI Delta Plan is essential
source_excerpt: a concrete roadmap to make the Netherlands a European leader in AI… We believe the AI Delta Plan is essential
source_text:
🇳🇱 Today, a broad coalition of AI experts presents the National AI Delta Plan – a concrete roadmap to make the Netherlands a European leader in AI through targeted investments in knowledge, talent, and infrastructure.

At KickstartAI, we strongly support this initiative and actively contribute to its vision. For years, we’ve been working hands-on with companies across sectors to move AI from experimentation to scalable impact, while cultivating one of the Netherlands’ biggest communities of AI professionals. 

📉 The Netherlands currently ranks 23rd worldwide in AI application. Without immediate and ambitious action, we risk falling further behind, with major consequences for our competitiveness, economic security, and technological sovereignty.

We believe the AI Delta Plan is essential as it calls for: 
 ✅ Accelerating AI adoption in businesses and government
 ✅ Investing in compute infrastructure and AI labs
 ✅ Attracting and training AI talent
 ✅ Supporting AI startups and scaleups
 ✅ Establishing an independent National AI Institute
 ✅ Addressing regulatory friction that limits AI adoption and startup growth 

We are committed to contributing to this mission by accelerating responsible AI adoption, fostering talent, and strengthening collaboration across the ecosystem. The time to act is now. 

👏 Shoutout to the team behind the strategy: Jelle Prins, Michiel Bakker, Renée Frissen, Stan van Baarsen, Oscar Lepoeter, Onno Eric Blom

Read more about the National AI Delta Plan below: 

https://lnkd.in/eGPw2fRz

#AI #AINetherlands #AIDeltaPlan

belief: AI initiatives should begin from clearly defined real-world problems rather than from the technology itself.
belief_statement: AI initiatives should begin from clearly defined real-world problems rather than from the technology itself.
source_quote: Start with the problem, not the technology
source_excerpt: Start with the problem, not the technology
source_text:
Very proud to see the CEOs of our founding partners featured in this insightful article by Het Financieele Dagblad!

📌 In this article, Frans Muller (Ahold Delhaize), Steven van Rijswijk (ING), Wouter Koolmees (Nederlandse Spoorwegen), and Marjan Rintel (KLM Royal Dutch Airlines) shared their lessons from deploying AI in large organizations: from reducing food waste and detecting unattended objects at train stations to fighting money laundering and predicting actual passenger numbers to better plan onboard services. 

What makes this piece special to us isn’t just the mention of KickstartAI, but the shared philosophy reflected: 

🔹 Focus over fragmentation
🔹 Start with the problem, not the technology
🔹 Don’t force AI on people, make it work for them
🔹 Combine operational reality with strategic vision
🔹 Move from isolated experiments to scalable practices

We’re proud of our partners, their honesty about what works (and what doesn’t), and how they keep pushing for AI that actually serves people, processes, and society.

👉 This is a must-read for any company navigating the shift from AI potentials to real operational impact. 

Read the full article below:
https://lnkd.in/erNQm2xt

belief: AI, and specifically NLP, can make a meaningful contribution to improving global food security.
belief_statement: AI, and specifically NLP, can make a meaningful contribution to improving global food security.
source_quote: How can AI support global food security in practice?
source_excerpt: How can AI support global food security in practice?
source_text:
🌎 How can AI support global food security in practice?

In our latest podcast, Evertjan Peer speaks with Marieke Meeske, PhD researcher at Zero Hunger Lab at Tilburg University, about how NLP is being used in food security analysis and policymaking. 

📊 They discuss her review of 60+ NLP use cases, covering early warning systems, public discourse around food sustainability, and why many AI solutions never move beyond the research stage.

They also touch on one part of her current research work with the UN’s Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO): using NLP to analyze policy integration across climate, agriculture, and nutrition — helping identify gaps and misalignments in national food policies.

🎧 Listen to the full conversation below and share your thoughts in the comments! 
https://lnkd.in/e79zVqjg
#AI #FoodSecurity #NLP #AIforGood #TheKickstartAIPodcast

belief: AI is fundamentally a positive force that can generate real impact for care, patients, and professionals.
belief_statement: AI is fundamentally a positive force that can generate real impact for care, patients, and professionals.
source_quote: At KickstartAI, we believe that AI is a force for good, and we’re proud to collaborate on innovations that have real impact on care, patients,
source_excerpt: At KickstartAI, we believe that AI is a force for good, and we’re proud to collaborate on innovations that have real impact on care, patients,
source_text:
We’re thrilled to start our project with Leiden University Medical Center to create an AI tool that improves wound care and reduces nurse’s administrative burden!

🩹 What we’re building
In collaboration with LUMC’s Cairelab and the Nursing Information Officers (NIOs), we’re developing an AI model that can generate structured wound reports directly from photos

💡 Why this matters
Nurses dedicate valuable time to wound reporting — a manual, time-consuming process, which often takes place daily. Our goal is to let AI handle the routine steps: automatically detecting the wound area, classifying tissue types, and drafting a structured TIME-style report that nurses can quickly review and validate.

The impact:
 ✅ Less administrative work
 ✅ More consistent and objective wound reports
 ✅ Better communication and handovers
 ✅ More time for direct patient care

The goal is to have the model operational in the hospital within one year. After validation, the same approach could be used in more hospitals across the Netherlands. 

This project builds on LUMC’s success as one of the winners of the National AI Challenge 2025, organized together with AI Coalitie 4 Nederland | AIC4NL.  As part of the prize, our team of ML engineers will help bring this idea to life — from prototype to adoption.

belief: Reducing nurses’ administrative burden is an important and desirable goal in healthcare innovation.
belief_statement: Reducing nurses’ administrative burden is an important and desirable goal in healthcare innovation.
source_quote: improves wound care and reduces nurse’s administrative burden!
source_excerpt: improves wound care and reduces nurse’s administrative burden!
source_text:
We’re thrilled to start our project with Leiden University Medical Center to create an AI tool that improves wound care and reduces nurse’s administrative burden!

🩹 What we’re building
In collaboration with LUMC’s Cairelab and the Nursing Information Officers (NIOs), we’re developing an AI model that can generate structured wound reports directly from photos

💡 Why this matters
Nurses dedicate valuable time to wound reporting — a manual, time-consuming process, which often takes place daily. Our goal is to let AI handle the routine steps: automatically detecting the wound area, classifying tissue types, and drafting a structured TIME-style report that nurses can quickly review and validate.

The impact:
 ✅ Less administrative work
 ✅ More consistent and objective wound reports
 ✅ Better communication and handovers
 ✅ More time for direct patient care

The goal is to have the model operational in the hospital within one year. After validation, the same approach could be used in more hospitals across the Netherlands. 

This project builds on LUMC’s success as one of the winners of the National AI Challenge 2025, organized together with AI Coalitie 4 Nederland | AIC4NL.  As part of the prize, our team of ML engineers will help bring this idea to life — from prototype to adoption.

At KickstartAI, we believe that AI is a force for good, and we’re proud to collaborate on innovations that have real impact on care, patients, and professionals 🤝 🇳🇱 

#AIinHealthcare #Healthcare #Innovation

belief: AI should be used as a force for social good, not just for commercial gain.
belief_statement: AI should be used as a force for social good, not just for commercial gain.
source_quote: ensuring AI is used as a force for social good
source_excerpt: ensuring AI is used as a force for social good
source_text:
🚀 Exciting news! We're kicking off the KickstartAI x ING Challenge: Financial Health Chatbot — a collaborative initiative focused on using AI to improve financial well-being.

Over the course of two weeks, 14 participants from our team and our partner companies — ING, KLM Royal Dutch Airlines, Nederlandse Spoorwegen, Ahold Delhaize, and bol — will join forces to co-create a smart, empathetic, and accessible GenAI-powered chatbot prototype.

💡 The core purpose of this challenge is to develop a tool that helps ING customers take control of their financial health, by supporting individuals experiencing financial stress or instability, as well as those actively seeking to improve their financial knowledge.

⚙️ We’ll build a solution that understands users and offers educational guidance based on validated ING content. By operating anonymously and steering clear of specific financial advice, the chatbot has the potential to lower the barriers to seeking financial support. Ultimately, it could increase financial literacy and resilience across society. 

The challenge will serve as a launchpad for validation and scaling, paving the way toward the implementation phase.

Together with our partners, we believe in sharing knowledge, building collectively, and accelerating AI adoption across the Netherlands — all while ensuring AI is used as a force for social good.🇳🇱

#innovation #GenAI #financialhealth #collaboration

belief: Using satellite imagery can help fill critical data gaps on clean water access and waste management in vulnerable areas.
belief_statement: Using satellite imagery can help fill critical data gaps on clean water access and waste management in vulnerable areas.
source_quote: explore how satellite imagery can help fill data gaps on clean water access and waste management — supporting more effective humanitarian action in vulnerable areas.
source_excerpt: explore how satellite imagery can help fill data gaps on clean water access and waste management — supporting more effective humanitarian action in vulnerable areas.
source_text:
🌟 Don’t miss our last workshop of the year!

We’re proud to partner with 510 - The Netherlands Red Cross' data & digital team for a hands-on mini hackathon to explore how satellite imagery can help fill data gaps on clean water access and waste management — supporting more effective humanitarian action in vulnerable areas.

🗓️ What's on the agenda: 
💧 Dive into satellite images to spot potential water points like wells or tanks.
♻️ Identify visible waste issues such as plastic accumulation or open dumpsites.
 🛠️ Work in mixed teams to explore what’s feasible from space, which datasets and tools are available, and how these insights could support humanitarian or environmental action.

If you’re interested in satellite data interpretation and humanitarian data challenges, this one’s for you. Join us for an evening of inspiring collaboration and use your skills to make a real impact!

Spots are limited. RSVP below 👇 

#Data4Good #SatelliteImagery #KickstartAI

🎧 In our latest podcast, Andrii Degeler (founder of unzip.media and journalist-in-residence at Dealroom.co) joins Ioanna Lykiardopoulou to discuss how LLMs’ data needs are shifting — and what that means for news publishers.

As Andrii explains, training data is no longer a viable revenue model; the focus is moving to real-time information and how its use is controlled and priced.

We also cover: 
🧠 AI literacy & media trust
🗞️ GenAI use in newsrooms 
 ✍️ Authorship and disclosure ethics
📊 The sustainability of media business models 

👉 Listen to the full podcast here: https://lnkd.in/ewnQpayp

source_text:
Our AI for the Planet meetup was a special one, bringing together the KickstartAI and Xomnia's Data&Drinks communities for an evening full of energy and lively conversations!

A big thank you to Xomnia for hosting us, to everyone who joined, and to our speakers who shared their work on stage: 
🔸 Akhil Chhibber 🇮🇳 🇳🇱 applying deep learning to map deforestation in the Amazon
🔸 Inês S. Veríssimo developing AI-powered tools to monitor and protect sea turtles.
🔸 Maria Bader, Ph.D. using satellite imagery to help wildlife rangers make data-informed conservation decisions.

Our key takeaways? 
🌱 Good data beats complex models.
 🧭 Tools need to be usable, not just accurate.
 💪 There’s always a way to create real social impact.

Couldn't make it? Join our upcoming meetup on December 11: https://lnkd.in/ecMy3ueV

#AIxPlanet #AIforGood #AI

belief: AI can and should be used as a force for good in society.
belief_statement: AI can and should be used as a force for good in society.
source_quote: At KickstartAI, we believe AI is a force for good.
source_excerpt: At KickstartAI, we believe AI is a force for good.
source_text:
🚀 We’re excited to kick off our project with Politie Nederland and start working on an AI tool to support the fight against environmental crime. 

While environmental crime is the third largest criminal activity in the world, the respective laws are complex and environmental criminals know how to cover their tracks. But in doing so, they’re prone to violating other laws. 

💡 That’s why the idea is to develop a tool that can search for all relevant legislation beyond the scope of environmental law, potentially uncovering criminal offences that are easier to prove. 

Think of Al Capone, who ultimately didn’t get convicted for the many murders tied to his name, but for something entirely different: tax evasion. 

The Police was one of the winners of the National AI Challenge 2025 we organized together with AI Coalitie 4 Nederland | AIC4NL. As part of the prize, our team of ML engineers will help bring their AI idea to life, from development to adoption. 

At KickstartAI, we believe AI is a force for good. We look forward to continue working with the Police on a project with such far-reaching societal impact. 

⚡ Stay tuned! 

#AIforGood #AI #AISocialImpact

🇳🇱 How can we strengthen the Dutch tech ecosystem and become a leader in AI?

That question took center stage at the Netherlands’ Tech Election Debate on October 7 — co-organized by KickstartAI, NLTech, and Techleap — where politicians, entrepreneurs, and experts came together to discuss how the Netherlands can build a future-proof digital economy and accelerate innovation.

The debate highlighted the importance of investment, talent attraction, and AI adoption as enablers of a strong digital future. 

At KickstartAI, we believe AI holds enormous potential to drive that future —  helping safeguard our welfare state, boost our economy, and strengthen our society. The urgency is clear: if we hesitate, the Netherlands risks falling behind in competitiveness and sovereignty.

💪 It’s time to act now, and do together. 

#AI #AIinNetherlands #TechDebate

source_text:
What does the GenAI shift mean for journalists and media organizations? 

🎧 That's they key question we explore in our latest podcast, where Ioanna Lykiardopoulou talks with Andrii Degeler, founder of unzip.media and journalist-in-residence at Dealroom.co. 

The discussion unpacks how newsrooms are experimenting with GenAI, the ethical questions around authorship and disclosure, the impact of Google's AI Overviews on publishers, and what it all means for the business models of media companies. 

👇 Listen to the full podcast below: 
https://lnkd.in/ewnQpayp

#TheKickstartAIpodcast #AI #journalism

belief: Open large language models should be transparent, multilingual, and aligned with shared European values.
belief_statement: Open large language models should be transparent, multilingual, and aligned with shared European values.
source_quote: open large language models, designed to be transparent, multilingual, and built around shared European values
source_excerpt: open large language models, designed to be transparent, multilingual, and built around shared European values
source_text:
🇪🇺 Europe’s betting big on open large language models, designed to be transparent, multilingual, and built around shared European values.

In our latest piece, we explore the current landscape of Europe’s open LLMs — from EU-backed initiatives to national projects that reflect different ambitions and constraints.

We interviewed Edwin Rijgersberg (founder of AI Studio Delta and the creator of GEITje, the first open Dutch LLM), who shared his  insights on how these efforts are progressing and what’s ahead for Europe’s open AI ecosystem.

Key topics include: 
🔸 The EU’s LLM approach
🔸 National initiatives and how they differ 
🔸 Where Europe stands against US and Chinese models 
🔸 Steps towards a pan-European infrastructure 

👇 Read the full article below: https://lnkd.in/euRsqtyC

#OpenLLMs #EuropeLLMs #EUAIstrategy

source_text:
🌎 How can AI and data science help us tackle the world’s biggest environmental challenges? Join us for a special edition of the KickstartAI meetup, co-hosted with the Data & Drinks community by Xomnia, to explore how technology is helping protect our planet.

➡️  What’s on the agenda:
🔸 Akhil Chhibber 🇮🇳 🇳🇱 (Lionsville B.V.): Mapping deforestation in the Amazon with deep learning
🔸 Inês S. Veríssimo (Xomnia): AI-powered solutions for sea turtle monitoring
🔸 Maria Bader, Ph.D. (CorrelAid Nederland): Using satellite imagery to help wildlife rangers make data-driven conservation decisions

Get ready for an evening of practical talks, networking, and drinks with fellow AI practitioners and environmental scientists.

👉 Interested? RSVP here: https://lnkd.in/euUuzrqg

#AI #KickstartAImeetup #AIfortheplanet

belief: AI is fundamentally a force for good rather than a blocker.
belief_statement: AI is fundamentally a force for good rather than a blocker.
source_quote: At KickstartAI, we believe AI isn’t a blocker — it’s a force for good.
source_excerpt: At KickstartAI, we believe AI isn’t a blocker — it’s a force for good.
source_text:
⚠️ What are we going to do to start AI adoption tomorrow?
That’s the key question our CEO Nils Beers posed during his speech at the Netherlands’ Tech Election Debate we co-organized last week.

At KickstartAI, we believe AI isn’t a blocker — it’s a force for good. It can safeguard our welfare state, boost our economy, and strengthen our society. The urgency is clear: if we hesitate, the Netherlands risks falling behind in competitiveness and sovereignty.

That’s why there’s no more time for endless roundtable talks, boardroom discussions, and debates in The Hague. 

It’s time to:
🔸Boost public investment by supporting frontrunners from business and from the tech ecosystem
🔸Adopt rules that support entrepreneurs instead of overburdening them with compliance and uncertainty
🔸Focus on measurable impact instead of AI pilots
🔸Create space for the doers and enable them to share their learnings

🚀 We need to act now. 
🤝 Let’s build a coalition of doing, together.

#AI #AIadoption #Coalitionofthedoing #AIadoptieNederland

belief: Collaboration among multiple organisations is a powerful driver for strengthening the Dutch AI ecosystem.
belief_statement: Collaboration among multiple organisations is a powerful driver for strengthening the Dutch AI ecosystem.
source_quote: the power of collaboration that can further boost the Dutch ecosystem
source_excerpt: the power of collaboration that can further boost the Dutch ecosystem
source_text:
We’re proud to have worked with KLM Royal Dutch Airlines on the Fuel AI Challenge ✈️ 

During an intensive two weeks, data scientists and AI engineers from KickstartAI and our partner companies, KLM, Nederlandse Spoorwegen, ING, and Ahold Delhaize, set out to explore: 

👉 How can AI help us better understand and predict the contingency fuel needed for each flight?

This challenge demonstrated not only AI's potential to bring added value, but also the power of collaboration that can further boost the Dutch ecosystem. 

Want to learn more about contingency fuel and what we discovered during the challenge? Check out the video below!

#AI #Innovation #Aviation

belief: A future-proof digital economy and strong tech ecosystem are essential priorities for the Netherlands.
belief_statement: A future-proof digital economy and strong tech ecosystem are essential priorities for the Netherlands.
source_quote: over een toekomstbestendige digitale economie en sterk tech-ecosysteem
source_excerpt: over een toekomstbestendige digitale economie en sterk tech-ecosysteem
source_text:
Afgelopen dinsdag organiseerden NLTech, KickstartAI en Techleap hét Nederlandse Tech verkiezingsdebat, waar kandidaat-Kamerleden en ondernemers in gesprek gingen over een toekomstbestendige digitale economie en sterk tech-ecosysteem.
 
Kandidaat-Kamerleden Wimar Bolhuis (GroenLinks-PvdA), Maes van Lanschot (CDA), Claire Martens - America (VVD), Joost Sneller (D66), Marieke Koekkoek (Volt Nederland) en Christian van der Krift, PhD (ChristenUnie) gingen het debat aan over hoe Nederland een toekomstbestendig tech ecosysteem —  van startups tot digitale kampioenen — kan bouwen en hoe we als Nederland AI-koploper kunnen worden.
 
Wat opviel tijdens de discussie over #kapitaal: vrijwel alle partijen pleitten voor meer langetermijndenken en investeringszekerheid. Niemand twijfelde aan de noodzaak om innovatie te stimuleren, maar de vraag bleef 𝘩𝘰𝘦. Er werd gepleit voor het versterken van de Europese #kapitaalunie, maar ook voor een nationale #investeringsinstelling en meer publiek-private samenwerking.
 
Onderwijs en kennisontwikkeling vormen de basis van onze toekomstige economie. Tegelijkertijd benadrukte de partijen dat fiscale regelingen als de expat-regeling cruciaal zijn voor het aantrekken van internationaal #talent, en dat aanpassing hiervan onverstandig is voor de Nederlandse concurrentiepositie binnen de EU.
 
#AI werd gezien als essentieel. VVD, GLPvdA, Volt, D66 en CU waren het eens: voor Nederland is het nu tijd voor actie om concurrerend te blijven en kansen te grijpen door snelle (en verantwoorde) AI-adoptie.

👉 Belangrijk is nu dat we deze mooie plannen en ambities terugzien in het regeerakkoord. 

#AI #AINederland

source_excerpt: Onderwijs en kennisontwikkeling vormen de basis van onze toekomstige economie.
source_text:
Afgelopen dinsdag organiseerden NLTech, KickstartAI en Techleap hét Nederlandse Tech verkiezingsdebat, waar kandidaat-Kamerleden en ondernemers in gesprek gingen over een toekomstbestendige digitale economie en sterk tech-ecosysteem.
 
Kandidaat-Kamerleden Wimar Bolhuis (GroenLinks-PvdA), Maes van Lanschot (CDA), Claire Martens - America (VVD), Joost Sneller (D66), Marieke Koekkoek (Volt Nederland) en Christian van der Krift, PhD (ChristenUnie) gingen het debat aan over hoe Nederland een toekomstbestendig tech ecosysteem —  van startups tot digitale kampioenen — kan bouwen en hoe we als Nederland AI-koploper kunnen worden.
 
Wat opviel tijdens de discussie over #kapitaal: vrijwel alle partijen pleitten voor meer langetermijndenken en investeringszekerheid. Niemand twijfelde aan de noodzaak om innovatie te stimuleren, maar de vraag bleef 𝘩𝘰𝘦. Er werd gepleit voor het versterken van de Europese #kapitaalunie, maar ook voor een nationale #investeringsinstelling en meer publiek-private samenwerking.
 
Onderwijs en kennisontwikkeling vormen de basis van onze toekomstige economie. Tegelijkertijd benadrukte de partijen dat fiscale regelingen als de expat-regeling cruciaal zijn voor het aantrekken van internationaal #talent, en dat aanpassing hiervan onverstandig is voor de Nederlandse concurrentiepositie binnen de EU.
 
#AI werd gezien als essentieel. VVD, GLPvdA, Volt, D66 en CU waren het eens: voor Nederland is het nu tijd voor actie om concurrerend te blijven en kansen te grijpen door snelle (en verantwoorde) AI-adoptie.

👉 Belangrijk is nu dat we deze mooie plannen en ambities terugzien in het regeerakkoord. 

#AI #AINederland

belief: AI should be translated from ideas and talks into practical, real-world applications and results.
belief_statement: AI should be translated from ideas and talks into practical, real-world applications and results.
source_quote: What excites us most is what comes next: turning these ideas into real collaborations and practical results
source_excerpt: What excites us most is what comes next: turning these ideas into real collaborations and practical results
source_text:
TechDays 2025 was a blast 🎉
This year was extra special, as we opened the doors to the entire Dutch AI community! A great crowd of AI engineers and data scientists joined our flagship event to bring our theme — AI in Action — to life 🧡

Here are some of our highlights:
🔹 We learned from insightful talks, including:
Edwin Rijgersberg on how (not) to train open LLMs
Maarten C. Stol on the relationship between learning and reasoning in modern AI
Okke van der Wal on how AI is transforming Uber payments

🔹We dived into: 
 – The future of AI with expert from TNO and ASML
 – AI’s social impact through inspiring use cases from Otermans Institute, 510 - The Netherlands Red Cross' data & digital team, and CorrelAid Nederland
 – Critical industry applications in healthcare (Ditto) and food waste prevention (OneThird)

🔹 We rolled up our sleeves in hands-on workshops — from building guardrails for conversational AI agents to designing multi-agent systems. Huge thanks to ML6 and Cisco!

🔹 We connected — through Q&As, networking, and over plenty of food and drinks, sparking new ideas and conversations.

✨ What excites us most is what comes next: turning these ideas into real collaborations and practical results, together with this incredible community. 

Two initiatives are already taking shape — and we can’t wait to share more soon. In the meantime, here’s a video recap to relive the spirit of TechDays 2025👇

#TechDays2025 #KickstartAI #AIinNetherlands

belief: AI tutors have significant potential to reshape education.

source_quote: In what ways can AI tutors reshape education?
source_excerpt: In what ways can AI tutors reshape education?
source_text:
In what ways can AI tutors reshape education?

🎧 In our latest podcast, Damian Ashworth from BrainBite and Ioanna Lykiardopoulou explore the potential of AI tutorship. 

They discuss, how  beyond offering personalization, AI can also support the "flipped classroom" model — where students first learn new material on their own and then use classroom time for discussion, problem-solving, and deeper engagement with the teacher.

👉 Listen to the full conversation here: https://lnkd.in/eWXnFrkx

#TheKickstartAIPodcast #AIinEducation #AI

source_quote: AI is moving fast into education, but what does it really mean for students and teachers?
source_excerpt: AI is moving fast into education, but what does it really mean for students and teachers?
source_text:
📚 AI is moving fast into education, but what does it really mean for students and teachers?

In our latest podcast, we speak with Damian Ashworth, teacher and co-founder of BrainBite – a platform that uses hyper-personalization and AI tutors to adapt lessons to a student's level, pace, and interests.

We talk about the benefits of AI tutors and personalized learning, the challenges and limitations that remain, and how a hybrid future of human teachers and AI support might look like. 

🎧 Listen to the full conversation below: 
https://lnkd.in/eGG9eR8K

#TheKickstartAIpodcast #AIinEducation #AItutors #AI

belief: Distilling and sharing lessons learned from projects is an effective way to help others succeed with AI adoption.
belief_statement: Distilling and sharing lessons learned from projects is an effective way to help others succeed with AI adoption.
source_quote: we distilled our experience into three core lessons – to help others bridge the gap from pilot to adoption
source_excerpt: we distilled our experience into three core lessons – to help others bridge the gap from pilot to adoption
source_text:
📊 We recently completed a project with Erasmus MC, where we turned an AI idea into a practical tool that predicts patient inflow in their busy emergency department.

Along the way, we saw firsthand why many AI projects stall in large organizations. The challenge is rarely the technology. It’s making the leap from a pilot to a solution that truly fits processes, strategy, and people.

That’s why we distilled our experience into three core lessons – to help others bridge the gap from pilot to adoption and unlock real value with AI.

👉 Curious which lessons can really make the difference? Read the full story below: https://lnkd.in/ewT7JeeX

#AI #AIadoption #KickstartAI

belief: AI is already a practical and current force in education rather than a distant future technology.
belief_statement: AI is already a practical and current force in education rather than a distant future technology.
source_quote: AI is no longer just a futuristic concept in education.
source_excerpt: AI is no longer just a futuristic concept in education.
source_text:
AI is no longer just a futuristic concept in education. In the Netherlands for example, NOLAI is piloting projects in schools to explore how AI can support teaching and learning.

In our latest article, we spoke with Eva de Schipper, Joep van der Graaf, and Johan Jeuring to dive into three specific projects: 

✍️ Self-regulation while writing
 📝 Semi-automated scoring for teachers
 📊 Progress tracking & task recommendations

These projects use methods and tools such as NLP for text evaluation, bayesian knowledge tracing to model progress, and clustering algorithms to group student answers. 

Together, they give us a glimpse into how AI may change everyday practice in schools — and the questions that still remain. 

👇 Read the full article below: 
https://lnkd.in/eM3RpM7X

#AIinEducation #KickstartAI #AI

belief: Building end-to-end AI pipelines from data anonymization to real-time deployment is an important capability for the organisation.
belief_statement: Building end-to-end AI pipelines from data anonymization to real-time deployment is an important capability for the organisation.
source_quote: built a full detection pipeline: from data anonymization and preprocessing to a labeling platform, YOLO-based object detection, and real-time tracking
source_excerpt: built a full detection pipeline: from data anonymization and preprocessing to a labeling platform, YOLO-based object detection, and real-time tracking
source_text:
🚀 PyData Amsterdam 2025 is a wrap! Here are some of our highlights:

🎤  A keynote by our Tech Lead Evertjan Peer on detecting unattended objects at train stations. As part of our project with NS Stations (onderdeel Nederlandse Spoorwegen), EJ showed how the team built a full detection pipeline: from data anonymization and preprocessing to a labeling platform, YOLO-based object detection, and real-time tracking.

🙌 Meeting new people at our community booth and connecting with fellow coders, data scientists, and AI engineers.

 🧩 Hearing about fresh techniques for RAG projects and cool Python notebooks, and exploring the many use cases for building on pre-trained foundation models.

#KickstartAI #AI #AIcommunity #PyDataAmsterdam2025

belief: Successful AI innovation depends more on people and governance than on technology alone.
belief_statement: Successful AI innovation depends more on people and governance than on technology alone.
source_quote: AI innovation isn’t just about technology. Prototypes and pilots are valuable but the real challenge – and opportunity – lies in people and governance.
source_excerpt: AI innovation isn’t just about technology. Prototypes and pilots are valuable but the real challenge – and opportunity – lies in people and governance.
source_text:
We're proud that our CEO Nils Beers represented KickstartAI at the Tweede Kamer during the roundtable discussion on the National Digitalization Strategy.

In his remarks, he emphasized an important truth: AI innovation isn’t just about technology. Prototypes and pilots are valuable but the real challenge – and opportunity – lies in people and governance.

🔸 Technology is rarely the barrier; adoption depends on how we bring people along.
 🔸 Pilots are valuable to test products, but true impact comes from scaling and embedding solutions.
 🔸 That requires clear strategies, strong governance, and above all, engaging stakeholders from the very beginning

Digital transformation in the Netherlands will succeed not only because of our technological capabilities, but because of our ability to collaborate, adapt, and change together. Now is the time to act. 

#Digitalisering #TweedeKamer #KickstartAI #AI

belief: Securing conversational AI with guardrails that block malicious content is an important and necessary practice.
belief_statement: Securing conversational AI with guardrails that block malicious content is an important and necessary practice.
source_quote: "secure conversational AI by designing, implementing, and testing guardrails that block malicious content from chatbot applications"
source_excerpt: "secure conversational AI by designing, implementing, and testing guardrails that block malicious content from chatbot applications"
source_text:
At TechDays 2025, we’re bringing two workshops you can’t miss if you’re building (or want to build) with conversational AI and multi-agent systems. 

⚙️ Workshop 1: 
Learn how to secure conversational AI by designing, implementing, and testing guardrails that block malicious content from chatbot applications. Approaches will include task-specific ML models, LLM-based solutions, and rule-based methods. 

⚙️ Workshop 2: 
Using AGNTCY’s CoffeeAgntcy reference implementation, you’ll build and run your own multi-agent system simulating a coffee supply chain. You’ll see a supervisor agent communicate with multiple agents via the SLIM protocol and even extend the setup by adding your own servers or components. 

The workshops will be led by ML6 | Your partner in AI (powered by Cisco) — who will share real lessons from the field and guide you through the hands-on labs. Cas Coopman, Cristóbal Sendín, Thomas Vrancken, Tom Istaz, Boaz Kwakkel

👉 Spots are limited! Register for TechDays 2025 and the workshops here: https://lnkd.in/e8Y9PJDA

👀PS: Attendance is free for AI professionals (valued at €1,000)

#TechDays2025 #AIinAction #AIworkshops

belief: Data-for-good projects in nonprofits can positively reshape a data scientist’s professional perspective.
belief_statement: Data-for-good projects in nonprofits can positively reshape a data scientist’s professional perspective.
source_quote: Can data for good projects in nonprofits reshape a data scientist’s perspective? According to Anastasiia Kulakova... the answer is yes.
source_excerpt: Can data for good projects in nonprofits reshape a data scientist’s perspective? According to Anastasiia Kulakova... the answer is yes.
source_text:
🧭  Can data for good projects in nonprofits reshape a data scientist’s perspective?

According to Anastasiia Kulakova, Data Scientist II at Kraken, the answer is yes. In our new Community Spotlight, she shares how volunteering at CorrelAid Nederland broadened her outlook — from seeing impact more directly, to adopting end-to-end thinking, to weighing tools and resources more carefully.

She also reflects on why transparency, responsibility, and stepping outside your comfort zone are lessons that carry back into her professional work.

👇 Read her full story below: https://lnkd.in/e3KH-cyW

#Data4Good #DataScience #SocialImpact

belief: Maximising return on investment from current AI research is an important objective for stakeholders.
belief_statement: Maximising return on investment from current AI research is an important objective for stakeholders.
source_quote: How do we get the best ROI from current AI research?
source_excerpt: How do we get the best ROI from current AI research?
source_text:
📣 TechDays 2025 speaker drop! Don’t miss the keynote by Maarten C. Stol,  Chief Scientific Officer at Braincreators. 

In his keynote, Stol will challenge us to rethink the choices that shaped today's AI. From the drawbacks of benchmark-driven AI research to the GPU hardware lottery of deep learning and the "bitter lesson" debate, he'll explore how things might have unfolded differently – and what that means for the future. 

He'll also tackle big questions: 
🔸 How do we get the best ROI from current AI research? 
🔸 How can entrepreneurs benefit from the AI boom beyond the hype? 
🔸 What might building a successful AI product look like in 2035? 

👉 Save your spot and register here: https://lnkd.in/e8Y9PJDA
👀 PS: Free access for AI professionals (valued at €1,000) 

#TechDays2025 #AIinNetherlands #AI

belief: Personalized learning supported by AI is a valuable direction for educational technology.
belief_statement: Personalized learning supported by AI is a valuable direction for educational technology.
source_quote: mastery-modelled recommendations for personalized learning
source_excerpt: mastery-modelled recommendations for personalized learning
source_text:
🔥 Our meetup on AI & Education is a wrap! A big thank you to everyone who joined us yesterday, and to Codam College for hosting the event! It was an insightful evening full of engaging talks and great conversations over drinks.

Here’s a quick recap of what we covered: 

🔹 Johannes Schleiss (Program Manager AI & Data in Education, SURF)
introduced SURF’s research on building a responsible AI ecosystem for Dutch higher education.
🔹 Nicholas Hogan (AI Team Lead, Futurewhiz) & Sumesh Dubey (AI Engineer, Futurewhiz) shared their journey in building mastery-modelled recommendations for personalized learning. They explained their pivot from traditional systems that hit a performance ceiling to building a conversational AI tutor. 
🔹 Ali Pala (co-founder, MyTaco AI) took us behind the scenes of the team's personalized voice tutors: from real-time, cross-browser voice interactions to semantic voice detection, multi-dimensional language assessments, and prompt engineering across six languages.

📣 Next up: TechDays 2025, our flagship AI conference. 
📅 October 02 
👉 Save your spot and register here: https://lnkd.in/e8Y9PJDA

#AIinEducation #KickstartAImeetups #AI #AIcommunity

belief: Open, locally relevant language models are strategically important for the Dutch AI ecosystem.
belief_statement: Open, locally relevant language models are strategically important for the Dutch AI ecosystem.
source_quote: GEITje-7B: the first open Dutch LLM. GEITje-7B was hailed as important step for Dutch AI
source_excerpt: GEITje-7B: the first open Dutch LLM. GEITje-7B was hailed as important step for Dutch AI
source_text:
📣 The speaker drop for TechDays 2025 begins 📣 

We're starting off strong with Edwin Rijgersberg, ML Engineer and the creator of GEITje-7B: the first open Dutch LLM. 

GEITje-7B was hailed as important step for Dutch AI and found its way into scientific papers, paving the road for a wave of Dutch open models. But about a year after its creation, GEITje-7B also became the first model removed under threat of litigation over its training data. 

Now, as the AI Act reshapes the field, Rijgersberg will take the stage to tell the story behind GEITje-7B's rise and fall — and share how (not) to train an LLM in 2025. 

👀 More speaker drops to follow! 
👉  Save your spot and register for TechDays 2025: https://lnkd.in/e8Y9PJDA
😉 PS: Attendance is free for AI professionals (valued at €1,000)

#TechDays2025 #AI #AIinTheNetherlands

belief: The Netherlands should remain a leading country in technology rather than fall behind.
belief_statement: The Netherlands should remain a leading country in technology rather than fall behind.
source_quote: hoe blijft Nederland koploper in tech?
source_excerpt: hoe blijft Nederland koploper in tech?
source_text:
🚀 Groei of Stilstand — hét Nederlandse Tech Debat
Op 7 oktober draait alles bij The Hague Tech om de vraag: hoe blijft Nederland koploper in tech?

NLtech, Techleap en KickstartAI organiseren hét verkiezingsdebat over de toekomst van het Nederlandse tech ecosysteem.

🇳🇱 Nederland hoort bij de digitale top van Europa. Maar de vraag is: houden we die voorsprong vast, of raken we achterop? En hoe gaan we van praten naar doen? 

Kandidaat-Kamerleden waaronder Claire Martens - America (VVD), Wimar Bolhuis (GroenLinks-PvdA) en Joost Sneller (D66) gaan de strijd aan over:
 
 👉 Hoe bouwen we een toekomstbestendig tech ecosysteem?
 👉 Wat hebben ondernemers écht nodig (talent, kapitaal, regels)?
 👉 Hoe maken we Nederland koploper in AI?

Reken op scherpe stellingen, pittige debatten en volop ruimte voor ondernemers en publiek om mee te doen.

 🗓️ 7 oktober 2025 | 15.00–17.30 (incl. netwerkborrel)
 📍 The Hague Tech, Den Haag
 
Kies voor groei, niet voor stilstand. Meld je nu aan en claim je plek hier aan:
🔗 https://lnkd.in/dNvzw6mR
Dit wil je niet missen!

belief: Scaling the detection of unattended objects is critical for ensuring safety at train stations.
belief_statement: Scaling the detection of unattended objects is critical for ensuring safety at train stations.
source_quote: Why scaling the detection of unattended objects such as suitcases is critical for station safety
source_excerpt: Why scaling the detection of unattended objects such as suitcases is critical for station safety
source_text:
Last week, Sophia Claire Zitman took the stage at Data Expo 2025 to share how KickstartAI and Nederlandse Spoorwegen are working together to improve safety at Dutch train stations using computer vision.

The session took the audience behind the scenes of a real-world case study:
🔸 Why scaling the detection of unattended objects such as suitcases is critical for station safety
🔸 How the idea turned into an operational AI solution
🔸 Technical hurdles of running vision models in a low-latency environment
🔸 Organizational & ethical challenges of using privacy-sensitive video data

The big takeaway? Responsibly deploying AI in high-stake settings isn’t about the technology. It’s about trust and collaboration, laying the foundation for broader AI adoption in the public domain.

#DataExpo2025 #AI #ComputerVision #PublicSafety

source_quote: "you’ve got the core of the Vesuvius Challenge. The result? A way to unlock texts unseen for millenia."
source_excerpt: "you’ve got the core of the Vesuvius Challenge. The result? A way to unlock texts unseen for millenia."
source_text:
What does it mean to “virtually unroll” a 2,000-year-old scroll?

Imagine a CT scan of a brain: layer by layer, more structures emerge. Now apply that idea to carbonised papyri, add segmentation software, and you’ve got the core of the Vesuvius Challenge.

The result? A way to unlock texts unseen for millenia.

🎧 We dive into it in our latest podcast with Cascha van Wanrooij, Evertjan Peer and Ioanna Lykiardopoulou

👉 Listen to the full conversation: https://lnkd.in/eWcaJkJx

#TheKickstartAIpodcast #VesuviusChallenge #AI

source_quote: our team will work closely with both LUMC and the Police to bring their ideas to life — from concept to fully working AI solutions
source_excerpt: our team will work closely with both LUMC and the Police to bring their ideas to life — from concept to fully working AI solutions
source_text:
✨ We have our winners! ✨

We’re thrilled to announce that Leiden University Medical Center (via its Cairelab) and Politie Nederland have won the National AI Challenge 2025, a unique collaboration between KickstartAI and AI Coalitie 4 Nederland | AIC4NL. 

🏆 Leiden University Medical Center impressed the jury with their idea to use AI to generate structured wound reports from photos, aiming to ease the administrative burden on nurses and improve the quality of care.

"We're super happy we won this challenge! We hope that  this solution becomes a standard support tool in the wound care process — available to every nurse, in every care setting," said Albert Simonse, Nursing Information Officer at LUMC. 

🏆 Politie Nederland stood out with their vision to use AI to cut through complex environmental legislation and help investigators leverage all available laws so that they can hold polluters accountable.

"I'm very happy that we won! This AI tool will greatly help strengthen our capabilities, enabling us to take down the biggest environmental criminals faster and more decisively," said Amir Niknam, Senior Advisor at the Police. 

🔜 Over the coming months, our team will work closely with both LUMC and the Police to bring their ideas to life — from concept to fully working AI solutions.

A huge congratulations to our winners, and a heartfelt thanks to our other outstanding finalists (Gemeente Den Haag, Instituut Verantwoord Medicijngebruik, Leiden University Medical Center via its Emergency Department) for their dedication and vision! 

👏 A big shoutout to our expert jury as well (Marelle van Beerschoten, Thomas Dohmen, Thomas Faber, Herman Kienhuis, Janneke Niessen) for their contribution! 

👉 For more info, check out our press release: https://lnkd.in/eKzhzawG

#NationalAIChallenge #KickstartAI #AIC4NL #AIForGood

belief: AI is a valuable tool for detecting ink in ancient carbonized texts such as the Herculaneum scrolls.
belief_statement: AI is a valuable tool for detecting ink in ancient carbonized texts such as the Herculaneum scrolls.
source_quote: How can AI help detect ink on 2,000-year-old carbonized texts?
source_excerpt: How can AI help detect ink on 2,000-year-old carbonized texts?
source_text:
💡 How can AI help detect ink on 2,000-year-old carbonized texts?

This question is at the core of the Vesuvius Challenge — a global competition to virtually unroll and read the Herculaneum scrolls buried by Mt. Vesuvius in 79 AD.

🎧 In our latest podcast, Evertjan Peer, Cascha van Wanrooij, and Ioanna Lykiardopoulou explain how a team of university students trained a model to detect ink by producing a segmentation map from the CT scans of the burned papyri.

But how did they make sure that the model wasn’t hallucinating? And why did they choose TimeSformer for training?

👉 Listen to the full discussion here: https://lnkd.in/eWcaJkJx

#TheKickstartAIpodcast #AI #VesuviusChallenge

belief: Reducing administrative and manual workload through AI is valuable because it frees professionals to focus on critical, higher-impact tasks.
belief_statement: Reducing administrative and manual workload through AI is valuable because it frees professionals to focus on critical, higher-impact tasks.
source_quote: reduce administrative tasks for housing inspectors, freeing up time for critical fieldwork.
source_excerpt: reduce administrative tasks for housing inspectors, freeing up time for critical fieldwork.
source_text:
The countdown is officially on! ⏳ We are just days away from selecting the winner of the National AI Challenge, a unique collaboration between KickstartAI and AI Coalitie 4 Nederland | AIC4NL.

Our five finalists have presented compelling real-world problems they believe AI can help solve. Their ambition to make a difference is truly inspiring:

- Gemeente Den Haag: Aims to use AI to analyze inspection photos and write reports in order to reduce administrative tasks for housing inspectors, freeing up time for critical fieldwork.
- Instituut Verantwoord Medicijngebruik: Envisions an intelligent engine to more efficiently screen medication incident reports, aiming to reduce manual work and provide faster, more widespread insights for healthcare organisations and professionals.
- Leiden University Medical Center (via its Cairelab): Seeks an AI-supported solution to generate structured wound reports from photos, aiming to ease the burden on nurses and improve the quality of care.
- Politie Nederland: Proposes an AI tool to cut through complex environmental legislation and help investigators leverage all available laws so that they can hold polluters accountable.
- Leiden University Medical Center (via its ED department): Envisions an AI-powered TriageTool that will allow patients to perform their own initial triage by filling in information on a (medical) device. This would enable a more objective and faster assessment, freeing up triage nurses to assist with other critical tasks.

The winner will receive a custom-built AI solution from A to Z, developed by our team. Our expert jury – Marelle van Beerschoten, Herman Kienhuis, Thomas Dohmen, Thomas Faber and Janneke Niessen — will make the final decision.

Stay tuned for the winner announcement next week, to find out which organization will have its AI solution brought to life! 

#NationalAIChallenge #KickstartAI #AIforGood #AI

belief: Scaling advanced AI and generative AI requires a fundamental, cloud-first architectural shift grounded in governance and security.
belief_statement: Scaling advanced AI and generative AI requires a fundamental, cloud-first architectural shift grounded in governance and security.
source_quote: "scaling advanced AI and GenAI requires a fundamental, cloud-first architectural shift built on principles of governance and security."
source_excerpt: "scaling advanced AI and GenAI requires a fundamental, cloud-first architectural shift built on principles of governance and security."
source_text:
A massive thank you to everyone who came out to our AI in Finance meetup last night! It was fantastic to talk tech, share a cocktail, and catch up. We're also very grateful to Eraneos Netherlands for partnering with us to make the event happen.

For those who couldn't make it, or for anyone who wants a recap, here are three key takeaways from the evening:

1️⃣ Infrastructure is the foundation: Federico Calore (ING) shared the blueprint for ING's next-gen data platform, VISTA, proving that scaling advanced AI and GenAI requires a fundamental, cloud-first architectural shift built on principles of governance and security.
2️⃣ AI for compliance is here: Gergely Bognár (Eraneos) demonstrated a GenAI framework that automates legal compliance checks for the Dutch pension market, ensuring both accuracy and completeness against legislation.
3️⃣ Transparency is non-negotiable: Tim Kleinloog (Deeploy) highlighted that in the fight against financial crime, AI models must be transparent, fair, and explainable to be truly effective and trustworthy in a regulated world.

💡 The big theme of the night was clear: we're moving from AI hype to practical, responsible implementation. 

It was a great way to close out the summer season, and now we're all fired up and ready to roll up our sleeves for the busy months ahead. See you all at the next one! 

Next up: 
AI x Education: Rewiring learning, Thursday, September 18 in Amsterdam

belief: The field is shifting focus from AI hype toward practical and responsible implementation.
belief_statement: The field is shifting focus from AI hype toward practical and responsible implementation.
source_quote: "we're moving from AI hype to practical, responsible implementation."
source_excerpt: "we're moving from AI hype to practical, responsible implementation."
source_text:
A massive thank you to everyone who came out to our AI in Finance meetup last night! It was fantastic to talk tech, share a cocktail, and catch up. We're also very grateful to Eraneos Netherlands for partnering with us to make the event happen.

For those who couldn't make it, or for anyone who wants a recap, here are three key takeaways from the evening:

1️⃣ Infrastructure is the foundation: Federico Calore (ING) shared the blueprint for ING's next-gen data platform, VISTA, proving that scaling advanced AI and GenAI requires a fundamental, cloud-first architectural shift built on principles of governance and security.
2️⃣ AI for compliance is here: Gergely Bognár (Eraneos) demonstrated a GenAI framework that automates legal compliance checks for the Dutch pension market, ensuring both accuracy and completeness against legislation.
3️⃣ Transparency is non-negotiable: Tim Kleinloog (Deeploy) highlighted that in the fight against financial crime, AI models must be transparent, fair, and explainable to be truly effective and trustworthy in a regulated world.

💡 The big theme of the night was clear: we're moving from AI hype to practical, responsible implementation. 

It was a great way to close out the summer season, and now we're all fired up and ready to roll up our sleeves for the busy months ahead. See you all at the next one! 

Next up: 
AI x Education: Rewiring learning, Thursday, September 18 in Amsterdam

belief: Applying AI and machine learning to cultural heritage problems like reading ancient texts is a valuable and worthwhile endeavor.
belief_statement: Applying AI and machine learning to cultural heritage problems like reading ancient texts is a valuable and worthwhile endeavor.
source_quote: an ongoing competition that combines ML, computer vision, and X-ray tomography to read 2,000-year-old scrolls
source_excerpt: an ongoing competition that combines ML, computer vision, and X-ray tomography to read 2,000-year-old scrolls
source_text:
🔥 What do AI, a volcano, and ancient texts have in common?

In our latest podcast, Evertjan Peer, Cascha van Wanrooij, and Ioanna Lykiardopoulou break down the Vesuvius Challenge — an ongoing competition that combines ML, computer vision, and X-ray tomography to read 2,000-year-old scrolls turned into charcoal by Vesuvius’ eruption in 79AD.

They discuss how the winning team of the Grand Prize 2023 improved virtual unrolling and ink detection, borrowing techniques from video models to revive lost words of the philosopher Philodemus of Gadara. 

🎧 History meets AI! Tune in to the full discussion: https://lnkd.in/eWcaJkJx

#TheKickstartAIpodcast #VesuviusChallenge #AI

belief: AI can help healthcare professionals better anticipate and manage unpredictable patient inflow and limited treatment capacity.
belief_statement: AI can help healthcare professionals better anticipate and manage unpredictable patient inflow and limited treatment capacity.
source_quote: How can AI help doctors and nurses better anticipate and manage patient inflow?
source_excerpt: How can AI help doctors and nurses better anticipate and manage patient inflow?
source_text:
Nine months ago, we kicked off an exciting journey with the Emergency Department (ED) of Erasmus MC, the winner of the National AI Challenge 2024. Together, we set out to tackle a critical issue: unpredictable patient inflow and limited treatment capacity.

Our question: How can AI help doctors and nurses better anticipate and manage patient inflow?

Over the course of the project:
🩺 Shadowed 5 shifts in the ED (in full nurse uniforms!) and interviewed dozens of doctors and nurses.
 🤝 Collaborated across 8 departments to combine medical, organizational, and technical expertise.
 📚 Hosted learning sessions to share insights and build AI knowledge inside the hospital.

The result? An AI-powered dashboard that:
✅ Predicts expected patient inflow in real time
✅ Provides long-term trends for smarter scheduling
✅ Helps staff shift from reacting to crises to managing resources proactively

For us, this project was about much more than building technology. It was about collaboration, empathy, and learning how AI can truly support healthcare professionals where it matters most: in patient care.

A big thank you to everyone at Erasmus MC and all the teams involved in making this happen. 👏 

#AI #NationalAIChallenge #KickstartAI

belief: Combining machine learning, computer vision, and X-ray tomography is an effective approach to reading otherwise inaccessible historical scrolls.
belief_statement: Combining machine learning, computer vision, and X-ray tomography is an effective approach to reading otherwise inaccessible historical scrolls.
source_quote: an ongoing competition where machine learning, computer vision, and X-ray tomography come together to read scrolls
source_excerpt: an ongoing competition where machine learning, computer vision, and X-ray tomography come together to read scrolls
source_text:
🔥 Our latest podcast matched summer temperatures as we moved from ashes to algorithms and how AI is resurrecting ancient 2,000-year-old texts.

How do you virtually unroll a scroll that would crumble to dust if opened? And how do you train a model to spot invisible, carbon-based ink?

🎙️ In our new episode, Evertjan Peer, Cascha van Wanrooij, and Ioanna Lykiardopoulou unpack the Vesuvius Challenge — an ongoing competition where machine learning, computer vision, and X-ray tomography come together to read scrolls buried in 79 AD after Mount Vesuvius’ eruption.

They break down how the winning team of the Grand Prize 2023 improved  virtual unrolling and ink detection, borrowing techniques from video models to revive the words of the philosopher Philodemus of Gadara, once thought lost.

📖 It’s a story of history meeting AI, with breakthroughs that could transform how we recover fragile texts across the world!

🎧 Listen to full podcast below:
https://lnkd.in/eWcaJkJx

#TheKickstartAIpodcast #AI #VesuviusChallenge

belief: The current industry practice of calling LLMs “reasoning models” is misleading.
belief_statement: The current industry practice of calling LLMs “reasoning models” is misleading.
source_quote: “Should we be calling LLMs "reasoning" models? ... the answer is no. ... the industry's label is misleading.”
source_excerpt: “Should we be calling LLMs "reasoning" models? ... the answer is no. ... the industry's label is misleading.”
source_text:
🤔 Should we be calling LLMs "reasoning" models?

Niya Stoimenova, who holds a PhD in AI reasoning, says the answer is no. In our new Community Spotlight, she explains why the industry's label is misleading. She breaks down deduction, induction, and abduction — and makes a compelling case for what's missing in today’s AI.

And the gap she points to? It may be the very thing that separates smart AI from real AGI. 

👉 This is a must read for anyone researching, building, or applying AI. 
📖 Read the full story below: 

https://lnkd.in/exucyzfz  

#AI #AGI #LLMs

belief: The EU AI Act should strengthen AI systems rather than slow them down.
belief_statement: The EU AI Act should strengthen AI systems rather than slow them down.
source_quote: So are we writing rules that strengthen these systems — or slow them down?
source_excerpt: So are we writing rules that strengthen these systems — or slow them down?
source_text:
⚠️ What if we went on an AI strike for just 48 hours?
No search. No logistics. No fraud detection. No diagnostics.

That’s the idea that Nils Beers breaks down in our latest podcast to demonstrate that AI isn’t hypothetical. It’s foundational.

And that’s exactly why the EU’s AI Act matters, and why it has to get this moment right. We’re not regulating an emerging technology anymore. We’re setting the rules for systems our economy and society already depend on.

So are we writing rules that strengthen these systems — or slow them down?

🎧 Listen to the full conversation (https://lnkd.in/eMXWnPE6) and share your thoughts in the comments!

#AIAct #AI #TheKickstartAIpodcast

source_text:
⏰ Upcoming meetup alert! On August 28, we're teaming up with Eraneos Netherlands to explore how AI is reshaping the financial sector. 

What's on the agenda: 

💶 AI for legal compliance in pensions: 
Gergely Bognár (Senior Data Scientist and GenAI Lead at Eraneos Netherlands) will walk us through an AI-based system he built that helps organisations check whether their internal policies meet legal standards. 

🕵 AI for fighting financial crime and the role of transparency: 
Tim Kleinloog (co-founder and CTO at Deeploy) will go into the details of how Bunq uses Deeploy to mitigate the risks that come with AI.

🏦 From strategy to VISTA — ING's next-gen data & analytics platform on public cloud: 
Federico Calore (Data Analytics Platform PAL for AI & BI at ING) will cover the principles behind VISTA, the key architectural choices, and lessons learned. 

PS: Cocktails included🍸 

Interested in joining us? RSVP below 👇 

#AIinfinance #AImeetups #KickstartAIcommunity

belief: The EU is currently lagging behind the US and China in key AI dimensions such as infrastructure, models, investment, startups, and adoption.
belief_statement: The EU is currently lagging behind the US and China in key AI dimensions such as infrastructure, models, investment, startups, and adoption.
source_quote: the EU is falling behind the US and China — in infrastructure, foundation models, investment, startups, and adoption.
source_excerpt: the EU is falling behind the US and China — in infrastructure, foundation models, investment, startups, and adoption.
source_text:
🇪🇺 Can Europe really achieve its AI ambitions?

If we look at the numbers, the EU is falling behind the US and China — in infrastructure, foundation models, investment, startups, and adoption.

And now, with the first obligations of the AI Act for general-purpose AI models officially in effect, the stakes are even higher. Across the tech sector, there’s growing concern that instead of accelerating innovation, these measures might be slowing it down.

In our latest podcast, Nils Beers and Ioanna Lykiardopoulou explore the real-world impact of the EU’s AI Act — and what it could mean for the future of AI in Europe.

🎧 Listen to the full discussion here: https://lnkd.in/eMXWnPE6
#AI #AIEurope #EUAIAct

In our latest podcast, Nils Beers and Ioanna Lykiardopoulou explore the real-world impact of the EU’s AI Act — and what it could mean for the future of AI in Europe.

🧠 Will AI ever develop its own will? 

A part of the AI safety community believes in instrumental convergence — the idea that AI will naturally develop common subgoals like self-preservation. 

In our latest podcast, our guest Paul Kuyer challenges this view. He argues we're more likely to see highly specialized AI tools rather than systems that suddenly develop their own emotions, desires, and intent. 

💬 What do you think? Share your opinion in the comments!

belief: Network analysis and LLMs are effective tools for mapping national threat landscapes.

source_quote: We explore how we used network analysis and LLMs to map the Netherlands’ threat landscape.
source_excerpt: We explore how we used network analysis and LLMs to map the Netherlands’ threat landscape.
source_text:
🚨 Our new technical blog is live!
 We explore how we used network analysis and LLMs to map the Netherlands’ threat landscape.

As part of our contribution to DenkWerk’s new report “Resilience by Design,” our ML engineer Cascha van Wanrooij built a node analysis tool to uncover how national threats interconnect, amplify each other, and where intervention can have the greatest impact.

In this post, we dive into:
🔹 Using LLMs to mine causal relationships from thousands of government and research documents
🔹 Applying centrality metrics to find the most influential risks
🔹 Creating an interactive graph for exploring systemic threats
🔹 Tacking the statistical challenge of rare events 
🔹 Why static websites with rich interactivity are the sweet spot for LLM-assisted development  

💡 Tip: Don’t underestimate smaller LLMs like Gemini 2.5 Flash — they’re fast, efficient, and very capable for this kind of semantic extraction work. 

👉 Read the full blog:
 https://lnkd.in/eBCG7BF3

belief: AI should not be treated as just another IT upgrade but as something that requires rethinking how organisations work and operate.
belief_statement: AI should not be treated as just another IT upgrade but as something that requires rethinking how organisations work and operate.
source_quote: "we have to stop treating it like another IT upgrade. We have to rethink how we work and operate."
source_excerpt: "we have to stop treating it like another IT upgrade. We have to rethink how we work and operate."
source_text:
💡 To unlock AI’s potential, we have to stop treating it like another IT upgrade. We have to rethink how we work and operate.

As Sophia Claire Zitman pointed out at TNW Conference, AI is too big to comprehend and too different in nature from what we’re used to. And that is why so many organizations struggle to turn potential into impact.

They’re blocked by misaligned incentives. They keep approaching AI as a technology rollout — when in reality, it’s a shift in mindset. It’s not about hiring more developers or running more pilots. Nor about using traditional metrics to measure success.

👉 It’s about the people who are ready to reimagine what’s possible — and act on it. That’s exactly what we do at KickstartAI.

#AI #AIadoptionNetherlands #KickstartAI

belief: Human intelligence is characterized by selectively ignoring most information and focusing on the small fraction that matters most.
belief_statement: Human intelligence is characterized by selectively ignoring most information and focusing on the small fraction that matters most.
source_quote: Humans evolved to ignore most information, and act on the small fraction that matters the most.
source_excerpt: Humans evolved to ignore most information, and act on the small fraction that matters the most.
source_text:
🧠 The smartest thing humans do? Ignore most of the data.

In our latest podcast, Evertjan Peer, Cascha van Wanrooij, and Paul Kuyer explore what sets human intelligence apart from AI — and why that difference matters in practice.

AI learns by consuming massive amounts of data. But humans evolved to ignore most information, and act on the small fraction that matters the most.

The contrast goes beyond how we process information. It also shapes what we prioritize, remember, and understand, which influences how we behave. The AI systems we’re currently using have different foundations:
🔎  Current AI architectures don't have an integrated, persistent (and autobiographical) long-term memory
⚙️ Current models operate without intent or self-awareness
📉 But they’re still being used to generate decisions and influence at scale

So what kind of guardrails do we need when deploying systems that lack the constraints and instincts that shaped human cognition?

#TheKickstartAIPodcast #AI #ArtificialIntelligence

source_excerpt: Current models operate without intent or self-awareness
source_text:
🧠 The smartest thing humans do? Ignore most of the data.

In our latest podcast, Evertjan Peer, Cascha van Wanrooij, and Paul Kuyer explore what sets human intelligence apart from AI — and why that difference matters in practice.

AI learns by consuming massive amounts of data. But humans evolved to ignore most information, and act on the small fraction that matters the most.

The contrast goes beyond how we process information. It also shapes what we prioritize, remember, and understand, which influences how we behave. The AI systems we’re currently using have different foundations:
🔎  Current AI architectures don't have an integrated, persistent (and autobiographical) long-term memory
⚙️ Current models operate without intent or self-awareness
📉 But they’re still being used to generate decisions and influence at scale

So what kind of guardrails do we need when deploying systems that lack the constraints and instincts that shaped human cognition?

#TheKickstartAIPodcast #AI #ArtificialIntelligence

belief: Current AI systems, including neural networks, lack essential traits of human cognition such as consciousness, wants, and desires.
belief_statement: Current AI systems, including neural networks, lack essential traits of human cognition such as consciousness, wants, and desires.
source_quote: the technology lacks the essential traits that define human cognition: consciousness, wants, and desires.
source_excerpt: the technology lacks the essential traits that define human cognition: consciousness, wants, and desires.
source_text:
🧠 Is our brain a biological computer?

In our latest podcast, Evertjan Peer and Cascha van Wanrooij sit down with Paul Kuyer to unpack a core analogy that sits at the heart of the AGI debate: If the human brain is a biological computer, why can’t a digital computer eventually do all the same things and reach superhuman levels?

While the premise is plausible, the logic behind it also risks being self-reinforcing — assuming the thing it needs to prove. 

Yes, neural networks are powerful. They outperform us in specific tasks. But, at the moment at least, the technology lacks the essential traits that define human cognition: consciousness, wants, and desires.

💬 Are we anthropomorphizing models just because they speak our language? Or do the similarities between brains and machines run deeper than we’re comfortable admitting? And if they do, what kind of caution does this call for? 

👉 Listen to the full discussion here:https://lnkd.in/eFGHnCyE

#TheKickstartAIpodcast #AGI #AI

belief: AI’s economic potential for the Dutch economy is very large but currently under-realised due to lagging adoption.
belief_statement: AI’s economic potential for the Dutch economy is very large but currently under-realised due to lagging adoption.
source_quote: "AI holds a €85bn potential for the Dutch economy, but... adoption is lagging."
source_excerpt: "AI holds a €85bn potential for the Dutch economy, but... adoption is lagging."
source_text:
AI holds a €85bn potential for the Dutch economy, but as Sophia Claire Zitman pointed out at TNW Conference, adoption is lagging.

It’s not about the technology, talent, or compute. It’s that AI is still stuck in the "potential" phase — too abstract, too big, too misaligned with how organizations actually work.

To unlock its impact, we have to stop treating AI like another IT upgrade. We have to rethink how we work and operate. We need to move from traditional metrics like OKRs and KPIs, to focusing on the people within organizations who are ready to reimagine what’s possible — and act on it.

In our projects, this approach has led to real results:
✈️  KLM Royal Dutch Airlines cut food waste by 65%
🛍️ bol improved parcel delivery efficiency by 6%
🚉 Nederlandse Spoorwegen train stations increasing safety 

👉 The potential is clear. The time to move from exploration to execution is now.

#KicstartAI #AIinNetherlands #AIadoption

source_text:
At yesterday's KickstartAI x BigData Republic meetup, we brought together the community to explore how EU's AI Act is moving from legal text to real implications for AI teams on the ground.

A big thank you to our speakers:
🔸 Kaan K. and Cas Mevissen (Policy Officers on AI, Dutch Ministries of Justice & Economic Affairs) for unpacking the Act’s core elements and sharing how the Dutch government plans to implement it.
 🔸 Jurriaan Parie (Algorithm Audit) for showing us what public ML audits look like in practice.
 🔸 Rens van Dongen (Nederlandse Spoorwegen) for sharing his insights on how NS is implementing an internal AI Management System to embed security and ethics. 

See you at our next meetup at the end of August! 

#EUAIAct #AIgovernance #AImeetup

source_text:
At the official launch of the Platform AI & Overheid yesterday, Sophia Claire Zitman and Rogier Klimbie spoke about KickstartAI and what it takes to set up a successful AI project. 💡 

It was an honor to connect with the Ministerie van Binnenlandse Zaken en Koninkrijksrelaties and ECP Platform voor de InformatieSamenleving, and to discuss with the participants what’s really needed to make AI work in practice, also within the government. 

Great to reconnect with Suzie Kewal, Karlijn van der Plaat, Nicolette Groeneweg-Faneyte and Sabine Herbrink. A big thank you to Martijn Bekking and Lex Dekkers. Looking forward to a follow-up!
